note_data.title: Petrochemicals and polymers
keyPoints: Filtration separates insoluble solid from liquid because liquid particles are small enough to go through filter paper pores while solid particles are too large
Crystallization is the formation of crystals from a cooling liquid or saturated solution to obtain solid from solid and liquid solution
Distillation obtains pure liquid from solution by heating the solution until it boils, turning pure liquid to vapour which is cooled by condenser back to liquid called distillate
Fractional distillation separates 2 liquids which are mixed (miscible) by heating mixture so liquid with lowest BP comes out first at top of fractionating column
In filtration, solid obtained is called residue and liquid obtained is called filtrate
A saturated solution means no more solid can dissolve in it
Crystallization process involves dissolving solid in solvent, heating solution to evaporate solvent producing hot saturated solution, then crystals of pure solid form on cooling
Distillation is possible because pure liquid changes to gas easily due to low BP while solid does not boil so remains in flask due to high BP
Chromatography separates mixture of organic compounds such as colour dyes

definitions.definition: 2 or more elements chemically joined together containing covalent or ionic bonds
2 or more substances which are not chemically joined together

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show the polymer structure with at least 3 monomer units and continuation bonds
Give the exact chemical name - polyethene not polythene

modelAnswer.question: Ethene can be converted into polyethene. (a) Name the type of reaction. (b) Draw the structure of polyethene showing at least 3 monomer units. (c) Give one use of polyethene.
modelAnswer.answer: (a) Addition polymerisation (b) [Structure showing -CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂- with continuation bonds] (c) Plastic bags / containers / bottles
markBreakdown: Mark 3: At least 3 monomer units shown

commonMistakes: Confusing when to use crystallization versus distillation - crystallization obtains solid while distillation obtains liquid
Not understanding that saturated solution means no more solid can dissolve in it
Mixing up residue and filtrate in filtration - solid obtained is residue, liquid obtained is filtrate
examinerTips: Any question that wants the method to obtain crystals, the process will be crystallization
The constant temperature of distillation process equals the boiling point of pure liquid
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Polymer structure showing repeating monomer units with continuation bonds (brackets with 'n' subscript) to indicate the polymer continues
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain the formation of polyethene from ethene, including the conditions required and the type of polymerization reaction
N/D 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — multiple choice question on identifying the monomer that forms PVC polymer from structural formulae
M/J 2022 P2 Q3c (4 marks) — students had to draw the structure of addition polymer formed from propene and explain why it's called addition polymerization
N/D 2021 P2 Q5b (3 marks) — question on uses of different polymers and relating their properties to their molecular structure
M/J 2021 P1 Q18 (1 mark) — identifying which hydrocarbon fraction is used as raw material for making polymers

workedExample.question: Ethene can be polymerized to form poly(ethene). (a) Draw the displayed formula of ethene (1 mark) (b) Name the type of polymerization reaction (1 mark) (c) Draw the structure of the polymer showing three repeat units (2 marks) (d) Give one use of poly(ethene) (1 mark)
steps: Step 1: Draw ethene showing C=C double bond with all hydrogen atoms: H₂C=CH₂
Step 2: Identify this as addition polymerization because monomers join by breaking double bonds with no other products formed
Step 3: Draw polymer structure showing brackets around repeat unit -CH₂-CH₂- with 'n' subscript, then show three connected units
Step 4: State a common use like plastic bags, bottles, or packaging materials
workedExample.answer: (a) H₂C=CH₂ (b) Addition polymerization (c) [-CH₂-CH₂-]ₙ or show three repeat units -CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂- (d) Plastic bags/bottles/packaging
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct ethene structure with double bond shown; 1 mark for 'addition'; 1 mark for correct repeat unit in brackets with n, 1 mark for showing linkage; 1 mark for valid use

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused addition and condensation polymerization' — always check if small molecules are eliminated; if not, it's addition polymerization
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often drew incorrect polymer structures without showing the repeat unit clearly' — always use square brackets around the repeat unit with subscript n
Examiner Report 2021: 'Candidates frequently gave vague uses for polymers' — be specific (e.g., 'plastic bottles' not just 'containers', 'carrier bags' not just 'packaging')
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw the polymerization of ethene showing: (1) Monomer structure H₂C=CH₂ with clear double bond (2) Arrow with conditions 'high pressure, catalyst' (3) Polymer structure with repeat unit [-CH₂-CH₂-]ₙ in square brackets (4) Show how double bond breaks and single bonds form between monomers. Include at least 3 repeat units when asked to show polymer structure.
note_data.quickRecap: Separation methods depend on the type of mixture: filtration for insoluble solid and liquid, crystallization to obtain solid from solution, distillation to obtain pure liquid from solution, and fractional distillation for miscible liquids. Each method works based on different physical properties like particle size, boiling points, and solubility.
